title: malty Egyptian fenugreek Wrap
description: A hearty, gooey, wholemeal flour wrap steamed to perfection with the comforting warmth of fenugreek and the sweetness of malted barley. Perfect for a solo dinner or a Buddhist temple-friendly meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine wholemeal flour, malted barley flour, and salt. Mix well until the flours are evenly combined.
Add in the fenugreek leaves, both fresh and dried, and mix until they are evenly distributed throughout the flour mixture.
Gradually add in the water, mixing until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal portions, depending on how large you want your wraps to be.
Roll out each portion into a thin circle, about 6-8 inches in diameter.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and cook the wraps for 1-2 minutes on each side, until they are lightly browned and crispy.
Steam the wraps over boiling water for 5-7 minutes, until they are soft and pliable.
Fill the steamed wraps with a mixture of fenugreek seeds, cilantro leaves, lemon juice, garlic, and cumin powder. Drizzle with ghee or vegetable oil.
Serve the malty Egyptian fenugreek Wrap hot, garnished with additional cilantro leaves and lemon wedges, if desired.
Store any leftover wraps in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
To reheat, simply steam the wraps for an additional 2-3 minutes, until they are soft and pliable.
